title: centaurea rhaetica subsp. ferdinandi
intro: Centaurea rhaetica subsp. ferdinandi, also known as Alpine Knapweed, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation due to its tannins and flavonoids content. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in the treatment of gastrointestinal problems, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as its antioxidant properties that may help protect against oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
